texto: That night I slept like a baby. When I woke the next morning I knew I was going to smoke heroin again. Everything that day was enjoyable: sitting on the bus, working all day – it all felt good. It was the best day of my life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Christine Lewry: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “That night I slept like a baby. When I woke the next morning I knew I was going to smoke heroin a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, addiction, memoir pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e boa-noite.

frase_original: That night I slept like a baby. When I woke the next morning I knew I was going to smoke heroin again. Everything that day was enjoyable: sitting on the bus, working all day – it all felt good. It was the best day of my life.

texto: Sitting cross-legged on her bed, I watch her take out her gear. She’s been smoking so much the room stinks of it. Over the last few weeks, I’ve seen her do it so often I’ve resisted the urge. It’s surreal, like I’m watching me from outside my body. My willpower is fragile at the best of times, but my resolve is always weaker in the evening.I feel a dread and a revulsion for what I’m about to do, but there’s a stronger feeling, an unutterable longing. I crack.‘Give us a line,’ I say.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Christine Lewry: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Sitting cross-legged on her bed, I watch her take out her gear.
